roof_eave_overhang_m.note: additional dimension callouts printed inside the main grid dimension chain -- '1.30' measured horizontally near the top (1-side and 4-side) and '1.50' measured vertically near the left margin (F-side and A-side) appear to describe the hatched roof rectangle's eave overhang relative to the 1/4 and F/A lines respectively, but the exact reference points (measured from the grid line itself vs. from the wall line) are not fully unambiguous at this resolution -- flagged for zoom-review rather than asserted with certainty.

roof_material: หลังคาเหล็กรีดลอน (corrugated steel roof sheeting), hatched with vertical rib lines across the whole roof plan

elements.description: Ø21/2"RD+RL combined symbol (circled dot) at the bottom edge of the roof plan, near 2, at a gutter/valley downspout point
elements.grid_ref: near 2, row A (bottom edge of roof)

elements.description: 'SLOPE 6°' vertical text label between 1-2, indicating roof pitch/slope direction toward a valley or eave
elements.grid_ref: near 1-2, spanning rows E through B

elements.description: second 'SLOPE 6°' vertical text label between 2-3, mirroring the first
elements.grid_ref: near 2-3, spanning rows E through B

elements.description: two curved arrow symbols (one near 1-2, one near 3) flanking the 'หลังคาเหล็กรีดลอน' label, likely indicating the corrugated sheet-metal fold/lay direction rather than a sanitary pipe
elements.grid_ref: near 1-3, row C-D area, at the roof label

elements.description: 4 rows of small cross ('+') marks aligned under grid columns 1/2/3/4, at rows E, D, C, and B -- appear to be background structural/purlin reference points carried onto this sanitary sheet rather than plumbing fixtures; not separately labeled with any pipe size or fixture symbol

warnings: This is a new sheet type not present in houses #3/#4's 10-sheet sanitary sets (which went straight from the upper-floor plan to the bathroom-detail sheet) -- pattern 'roof_plan' is still draft per schema doc section 1/12, so the field-set here is a best-effort adaptation of the 'plan' pattern's fields (grid, elements) plus roof-specific additions (roof_material, roof slope labels), not a previously-verified template.

This sheet shows only ONE roof drain/rainwater-leader point (Ø21/2"RD+RL) despite the upper-floor plan (page53) showing 2 separate Ø21/2"RFD+RL points on its own small 'หลังคา ค.ส.ล.' (RC roof canopy) area -- those two RC-canopy drains are a distinct, smaller architectural feature from this main corrugated-steel roof plan and are not expected to coincide 1:1; not treated as a discrepancy, just noted for cross-reference.

The exact reference points for the '1.30' (horizontal) and '1.50' (vertical) eave-overhang-style dimensions relative to the main 1/4/F/A lines are ambiguous at this resolution -- described descriptively rather than asserted as precise offsets; flagged for zoom-review.

The two curved 'fold direction' arrows and the 4 rows of small cross marks at grid intersections are likely roofing/structural reference marks rather than sanitary fixtures (no pipe size, no fixture symbol, no legend match on this house's SN-01 symbol legend) -- included for completeness per the 'inventory every element' rule but flagged as possibly outside the sanitary discipline's own scope.
Overall grid (1,2,3,4 x F,E,D,C,B,A) and dimension chain confirmed identical to the ground-floor (page52) and upper-floor (page53) plans and the structural grid master.
